source_name: Code4rena Althea Liquid Infrastructure Findings and Analysis Report (2024-02)
finding_title: Distribution can be bricked, and double claims by a few holders are possible when owner calls LiquidInfrastructureERC20::setDistributableERC20s
summary: Double claim, DOS by bricking distribution, few holders can lose some rewards due to missing validation of distribution timing when owner is calling LiquidInfrastructureERC20::setDistributableERC20s.
root_cause: permissionless denial-of-service condition in Distribution can be bricked, and double claims by a few holders are possible when owner calls LiquidInfrastructureERC20::setDistributableERC20s, allowing one actor to block a required user or protocol operation
broken_invariants: reward and distribution accounting must preserve each participant’s accrued value under the root cause: permissionless denial-of-service condition in Distribution can be bricked, and double claims by a few holders are possible when owner calls LiquidInfrastructureERC20::setDistributableERC20s, allowing one actor to block a required user or protocol operation
users must retain a reliable exit path unless an explicitly designed and validated pause or restriction is active
reward and distribution accounting must preserve each participant’s accrued value across claims, transfers, and configuration changes
attack_preconditions: the affected component is deployed or configured in the way described by the final report
the report-described user, keeper, vault, connector, oracle, or liquidation flow is reachable in the audited code version
attacker_capability: external user, keeper, liquidator, vault participant, or protocol-integrated actor able to trigger the affected flow
trigger_actions: execute the report-described function sequence or state transition
observe mispricing, accounting drift, value loss, fund lock, or denial of service

impact.description: Double claim, DOS by bricking distribution, few holders can lose some rewards due to missing validation of distribution timing when owner is calling LiquidInfrastructureERC20::setDistributableERC20s.

why_not_higher: final C4 severity is Medium; exploitability, affected value, or preconditions are bounded relative to High
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with public report evidence and affected code/finding references
common_false_positive_angles: verify the same component and configuration are in scope
check whether the issue is reachable by an untrusted or economically motivated actor
confirm the impact is more than a view-only mismatch, dust loss, or privileged-only misconfiguration
mitigations: Modify LiquidInfrastructureERC20::setDistributableERC20s to only change the desirable tokens after a potential distribution for fair reward sharing. function setDistributableERC20s( address[] memory _distributableERC20s ) public onlyOwner { + require(!_isPastMinDistributionPeriod(), "set only just after distribution"); distributableERC20s = _distributableERC20s; }
